Living Conditions.desc: Proportion of people using improved sanitation facilities that are not shared with other households and where excreta are safely disposed in situ or transported and treated off-site.
Living Conditions.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Average age of women at marriage
Living Conditions.desc: Average age of women who got married for the first time that year.
Living Conditions.source: OECD (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: How important family is to people in life
Living Conditions.desc: % of respondents replying "very important" when asked "For each of the following aspects, indicate how important it is in your life. Would you say it is very important, rather important, not very important or not important at all?" in the item "Family".
Living Conditions.source: Integrated Values Surveys (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Number of people with and without access to clean cooking fuels
Living Conditions.desc: Access to clean fuels or technologies such as natural gas, electricity, and clean cookstoves reduces exposure to indoor air pollutants, a leading cause of death in low-income households.
Living Conditions.source: World Health Organization (via World Bank) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Share of people who say they are happy
Living Conditions.desc: % of respondents replying "very happy" or "quite happy" when asked "Taking all things together, would you say you are very happy, quite happy, not very happy or not at all happy?"
Living Conditions.source: Integrated Values Surveys (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Share of population not using improved sanitation facilities
Living Conditions.desc: If no improved sanitation facility is available, people might practice open defecation or use unimproved sanitation facilities.
Living Conditions.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Using safely managed drinking water in urban vs. rural areas
Living Conditions.desc: Proportion of people using an improved drinking water source that is located on premises, available when needed and free from faecal and priority chemical contamination.
Living Conditions.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Gender Development Index
Living Conditions.desc: The Gender Development Index (GDI) measures gender inequalities in the achievement of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living.
Living Conditions.source: UNDP, Human Development Report (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: How important religion is to people in life
Living Conditions.desc: % of respondents replying "very important" when asked "For each of the following aspects, indicate how important it is in your life. Would you say it is very important, rather important, not very important or not important at all?" in the item "Religion".
Living Conditions.source: Integrated Values Surveys (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Marriage for same-sex partners
Living Conditions.desc: Legislation status of same-sex marriage. This is marriage and marriage recognition between two people of the same biological sex and/or gender identity.
Living Conditions.source: Equaldex (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Number of people without access to clean fuels for cooking
Living Conditions.desc: Access to clean fuels or technologies such as natural gas, electricity, and clean cookstoves reduces exposure to indoor air pollutants, a leading cause of death in low-income households.
Living Conditions.source: World Health Organization (via World Bank) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Sanitation facilities usage
Living Conditions.desc: Open defecation includes defecating in fields, forests, bushes, bodies of water or other open spaces, not using any sanitation facilities.
Living Conditions.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Share using safely managed drinking water
Living Conditions.desc: Proportion of people using an improved drinking water source that is located on premises, available when needed and free from faecal and priority chemical contamination.
Living Conditions.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Trust in institutions
Living Conditions.desc: % of respondents replying that they "trust completely" or "trust somewhat" when asked "I am going to name a number of organizations. For each one, could you tell me how much confidence you have in them: is it a great deal of confidence, quite a lot of confidence, not very much confidence or none at all?" in the organization "The justice system (courts)".
Living Conditions.source: Integrated Values Surveys (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Share of the population with access to clean fuels for cooking
Living Conditions.source: World Health Organization - Global Health Observatory (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Democracy
Rights & Democracy.desc: Identifies the political regime of a country by distinguishing between closed autocracies, electoral autocracies, electoral democracies, and liberal democracies.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Human Rights Index
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which people are free from government torture, political killings, and forced labor, they have property rights, and enjoy the freedoms of movement, religion, expression, and association.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of people who said they were asked for a bribe or paid one
Rights & Democracy.desc: Share of respondents who reported having contact with a public official in the past 12 months and paying or being asked to pay a bribe.
Rights & Democracy.source: United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of senior and middle management positions filled by women
Rights & Democracy.desc: Proportion of women in senior and middle management positions - 13th ICLS (%)
Rights & Democracy.source: International Labour Organization – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Tax revenue as share of GDP
Rights & Democracy.desc: Taxes are mandatory payments to the government that are not made in exchange for a specific good or service.
Rights & Democracy.source: UNU-WIDER Government Revenue Dataset (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Gender Inequality Index
Rights & Democracy.desc: This index captures gender-based disadvantage in reproductive health, empowerment and the labour market; ranges from 0 (full equality) to 1 (maximum inequality), representing the share of potential human development lost because of gender gaps.
Rights & Democracy.source: UNDP, Human Development Report (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Percentage of territory effectively controlled by government
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the percentage of territory controlled by the government.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of firms that report being asked to pay a bribe
Rights & Democracy.desc: Share of firms that were asked to pay a bribe when dealing with core public services, such as operating licenses and construction permits.
Rights & Democracy.source: World Bank Enterprise Surveys (2024) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of women in parliament
Rights & Democracy.desc: Percentage of women in the lower or single chamber of the legislature.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Average weekly working hours of children
Rights & Democracy.desc: Average weekly hours worked by children aged 7-14 years involved in economic activity and attending school.
Rights & Democracy.source: International Labour Organization, UNICEF, and World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Electoral Democracy Index
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which political leaders are elected under comprehensive suffrage in free and fair elections, and freedoms of association and expression are guaranteed.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Political Corruption Index
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which a country is affected by political corruption.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Rigorous and Impartial Public Administration Score
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which public officials respect the law, and administer it without arbitrariness and bias.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of firms with a female top manager
Rights & Democracy.desc: Top manager refers to the highest ranking manager or CEO of the establishment. This may be the owner if she works as the manager of the firm.
Rights & Democracy.source: World Bank – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of women who have undergone female genital mutilation
Rights & Democracy.source: UNICEF, Demographic and Health Surveys (DHS), and Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys (MICS), via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Women's Political Empowerment Index
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which women enjoy civil liberties, can participate in civil society, and are represented in politics.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Freedom of Expression Index
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which people can discuss political matters at home and in the public sphere, the press, and media are free and can present different political perspectives, and the freedom of academic and cultural expression.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Gender wage gap
Rights & Democracy.desc: Difference between average earnings of men and average earnings of women expressed as a percentage of average earnings of men.
Rights & Democracy.source: International Labour Organization (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Liberal Democracy Index
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent of suffrage, the freedom and fairness of elections, freedoms of association and expression, individual and minority rights, equality before the law, and executive constraints.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Same-sex sexual acts legal
Rights & Democracy.desc: Legal status of consensual homosexual acts among adults in private. The categories are "Legal" and "Illegal".
Rights & Democracy.source: Mignot (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of children in employment vs. GDP per capita
Rights & Democracy.desc: Share of children aged 7-14 years involved in economic activity for at least one hour in the reference week of the survey.
Rights & Democracy.source: International Labour Organization, UNICEF, and World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of people who say accepting a bribe is never justifiable
Rights & Democracy.desc: % of respondents replying 1 in a scale from 1 to 10 when asked "Please tell me for each of the following statements whether you think it can always be justified, never be justified, or something in between, using this card", when the statement was "Accepting a bribe in the course of their duties". In the scale, 1 means "never justifiable" and 10 means "always justifiable".
Rights & Democracy.source: Integrated Values Surveys (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of women who were married by age 15
Rights & Democracy.desc: Proportion of women aged 20-24 years who were married or in a union before age 15 (%)
Rights & Democracy.source: UNICEF – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Marriage for same-sex partners
Rights & Democracy.desc: Legislation status of same-sex marriage. This is marriage and marriage recognition between two people of the same biological sex and/or gender identity.
Rights & Democracy.source: Equaldex (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Gender marker can be legally changed
Rights & Democracy.desc: Legislation status of the right to change legal gender. This is the legal recognition of sex reassignment by permitting a change of legal gender on an individual's birth certificate.
Rights & Democracy.source: Equaldex (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: State Capacity Index
Rights & Democracy.desc: Measures state capacity by combining 21 different indicators related to three key dimensions: extractive capacity, coercive capacity, and administrative capacity. Higher values indicate greater state capacity.
Rights & Democracy.source: Jonathan Hanson and Rachel Sigman (2021); Population based on various sources (2023) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Third gender legally recognized
Rights & Democracy.desc: Legal recognition of non-binary, genderqueer, or third gender identities.
Rights & Democracy.source: Equaldex (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Number of terrorist attacks

Violence & War.label: Percentage of territory effectively controlled by government

Violence & War.label: Armed forces personnel

Violence & War.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024); United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Rigorous and Impartial Public Administration Score
Violence & War.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which public officials respect the law, and administer it without arbitrariness and bias.

Violence & War.label: Share of women who have undergone female genital mutilation
Violence & War.source: UNICEF, Demographic and Health Surveys (DHS), and Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys (MICS), via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Terrorist attacks by region

Violence & War.label: Deaths in armed conflicts by region
Violence & War.desc: The best estimate of the number of deaths of combatants and civilians due to fighting in interstate, intrastate, extrasystemic, non-state conflicts, and one-sided violence that were ongoing that year.

Violence & War.label: Share of women who were married by age 15

Population & Demography.label: Child mortality rate
Population & Demography.desc: The long-run estimated share of newborns who die before reaching the age of five.
Population & Demography.source: Gapminder (2015); UN Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Fertility rate: births per woman
Population & Demography.desc: The total fertility rate: the average number of children a woman would have if she experienced the year's age-specific birth rates throughout her childbearing years.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Life expectancy
Population & Demography.desc: Period life expectancy is the number of years the average person born in a certain year would live if they experienced the same chances of dying at each age as people did that year.
Population & Demography.source: Riley (2005); Zijdeman et al. (2015); HMD (2025); UN WPP (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Population
Population & Demography.desc: The total population, measured on 1 July of the year shown.

Population & Demography.label: Population by age group
Population & Demography.desc: The total population, measured on 1 July of the year shown. This only includes people aged 65+.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Sex ratio at birth
Population & Demography.desc: The number of male births per 100 female births.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Share of population living in urban areas
Population & Demography.desc: Share of the population living in areas classified as urban, based on each country's national definition.

Population & Demography.label: Share of the population born in another country

Population & Demography.label: Fertility rate: births per woman
Population & Demography.desc: The average number of live births a hypothetical cohort of women would have at the end of their reproductive period if they were subject during their whole lives to the fertility rates of a given period and if they were not subject to mortality.
Population & Demography.source: Human Fertility Database (2025); UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Infant mortality rate
Population & Demography.desc: The estimated number of deaths of children aged under one year, per 100 live births .
Population & Demography.source: United Nations Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Life expectancy
Population & Demography.desc: The period life expectancy at birth among totals, in a given year.
Population & Demography.source: Human Mortality Database (2025); UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Number of people living in urban and rural areas
Population & Demography.desc: Number of people living in areas classified as urban, based on each country's national definition.
Population & Demography.source: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Population by age group
Population & Demography.desc: The total population, measured on 1 July of the year shown. This only includes people aged 0-4.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Population growth rate
Population & Demography.desc: The annual rate at which the population is growing, expressed as a percentage.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Refugees by country of asylum
Population & Demography.desc: People who fled their country of origin because of serious threats against which the authorities of their home country cannot or will not protect them. This includes all refugees who live in this country, not just those who arrived that year.
Population & Demography.source: UNHCR (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Share of the population that is female

Population & Demography.label: Birth rate
Population & Demography.desc: The total number of births per 1,000 people in a given year.

Population & Demography.label: Births and deaths per year
Population & Demography.desc: The number of deaths in a calendar year.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Life expectancy of women vs. men
Population & Demography.desc: Period life expectancy at birth.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Median age
Population & Demography.desc: The age that divides the population into two halves of equal size — half are younger and half are older.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Refugees by country of origin
Population & Demography.desc: People who fled their country of origin because of serious threats against which the authorities of their home country cannot or will not protect them. This includes all refugees, not just those who fled that year.
Population & Demography.source: UNHCR (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Share of population living in urban and rural areas
Population & Demography.desc: Share of the population living in areas classified as urban, based on each country's national definition.
Population & Demography.source: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Total dependency ratio
Population & Demography.desc: The number of children (under 15) and people aged 65 and over per 100 working-age people (aged 15 to 64).

Population & Demography.label: Youth mortality rate
Population & Demography.desc: The estimated number of deaths of children aged under fifteen years, per 100 live births.

Population & Demography.label: Average age of mothers at childbirth
Population & Demography.desc: Mean age of mothers at the birth of their children if women were subject throughout their lives to the age-specific fertility rates observed in a given year.

Population & Demography.label: Number of children, working-age adults, and older people

Population & Demography.label: Number of people living in cities, towns and rural areas

Population & Demography.label: Sex gap in life expectancy

Population & Demography.label: Total number of international immigrants

Population & Demography.label: Population
